deep_dive.title: COSM: A Cooperative Scheduling Framework for Concurrent PIM and CPU Execution on Mobile Devices
deep_dive.abstract: The development of on-device large language models (LLMs) is driven by the need for privacy and fast response times. Energy-intensive data transfer on mobile devices makes Processing-in-Memory (PIM) an effective solution. Due to stringent DRAM cost constraints, limited physical footprint on circuit boards, and the interaction between applications and LLMs, it is imperative for the CPU and PIM to operate concurrently within a shared memory space. However, challenges such as bank conflicts and bus congestion can arise, potentially diminishing the performance and energy benefits of PIM. To address this challenge, we introduce COSM, a cooperative scheduling framework designed to facilitate the concurrent operation of PIM and CPU tasks on mobile platforms. Our key innovations include: 1) a low-interference PIM control interface that generates the maximum number of PIM commands without disrupting CPU memory accesses; 2) an idleness-aware scheduling method that integrates PIM commands into available idle time windows within the CPU's access sequence. COSM not only hides PIM execution latency from the CPU, but also overlaps PIM execution with data transfer. Experiments on concurrent execution of LLMs and mobile workloads, including mobile applications and compute-intensive kernels, demonstrate that COSM improves PIM throughput by up to 2.8x compared to the baseline scheduling method with less than 2.0% CPU performance loss.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Cooperative scheduling framework for concurrent PIM and CPU execution on mobile devices
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向移动设备上PIM和CPU并发执行的协同调度框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向移动设备上PIM和CPU并发执行的协同调度框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向移动设备上PIM和CPU并发执行的协同调度框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces COSM, a cooperative scheduling framework enabling concurrent PIM and CPU operation on mobile devices within shared memory space, addressing bank conflicts and bus congestion. It preserves PIM's performance and energy benefits for on-device LLM inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 移动设备上运行大语言模型需要PIM以减少数据传输能耗，但PIM与CPU共享内存时存在存储体冲突和总线拥塞等问题。本文提出COSM协同调度框架，实现PIM与CPU在共享内存空间中的高效并发执行，避免性能退化。该框架针对移动设备DRAM成本约束设计，保障了PIM带来的性能和能效优势，推动了近存计算在移动端的实用化。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 移动设备上运行大语言模型需要PIM以减少数据传输能耗，但PIM与CPU共享内存时存在存储体冲突和总线拥塞等问题。本文提出COSM协同调度框架，实现PIM与CPU在共享内存空间中的高效并发执行，避免性能退化。该框架针对移动设备DRAM成本约束设计，保障了PIM带来的性能和能效优势，推动了近存计算在移动端的实用化。

deep_dive.title: In-situ Indexing via Memristive Content-Addressable Memory
deep_dive.abstract: Processing-in-Memory (PIM) is a proven paradigm for overcoming the ``memory wall". However, while data indexing is severely bottlenecked by this same wall, it remains unclear how indexing can effectively benefit from PIM's unique capabilities. We present PATH, an in-situ indexing architecture that bridges this gap by leveraging the massive parallelism and inherent data-movement of PIMs. Specifically, we first reformulate the fundamental indexing operations, namely Insert, Search, Update, and Delete, into highly parallel in-situ content-addressable memory operations executed directly within memory arrays. Taking hash indexes as a typical case, we elaborate how PATH breaks the inherent trade-off among memory accesses, load factor, and process latency in conventional hashing schemes. By adopting ultra-large logical buckets and in-memory moving, PATH virtually eliminates the cost of hash collision resolution and significantly reduces resizing overhead. Compared with state-of-the-art schemes, PATH achieves $4.7-7.8\times$ higher throughput, $>14.5\times$ lower tail latency, and $>61.4\%$ fewer memory accesses under insertions, laying a scalable foundation for next-generation data-centric computing.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: In-situ indexing architecture leveraging PIM and content-addressable memory
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用处理-存内和内容可寻址存储器的原位索引架构
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用处理-存内和内容可寻址存储器的原位索引架构
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用处理-存内和内容可寻址存储器的原位索引架构
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents PATH, an in-situ indexing architecture that leverages the massive parallelism of Processing-in-Memory to perform indexing operations (Insert, Search, Update, Delete) directly within memory arrays via content-addressable memory operations, breaking the trade-off among memory accesses, load factor, and latency for hash indexes.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 传统索引受限于“存储墙”。本文提出PATH原位索引架构，利用处理-存内（PIM）的大规模并行性，将索引操作（插入、搜索、更新、删除）转化为内容可寻址存储器操作，直接在存储阵列内执行。以哈希索引为例，PATH打破了内存访问、负载因子和延迟之间的固有权衡，展示了PIM在数据索引中的新应用方式，对近存计算系统设计具有启发意义。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 传统索引受限于“存储墙”。本文提出PATH原位索引架构，利用处理-存内（PIM）的大规模并行性，将索引操作（插入、搜索、更新、删除）转化为内容可寻址存储器操作，直接在存储阵列内执行。以哈希索引为例，PATH打破了内存访问、负载因子和延迟之间的固有权衡，展示了PIM在数据索引中的新应用方式，对近存计算系统设计具有启发意义。
